answer.content:
Here is an example of an HTML file that demonstrates the concept of time complexity: ``` Time Complexity

Time Complexity

The time complexity of an algorithm depends on how many operations it performs during execution. In general, the more operations an algorithm performs, the higher its time complexity will be.

For example, consider the following algorithm:

  • Divide two numbers
  • Add the results together
  • Print the result

The time complexity of this algorithm is O(n^2), where n is the number of elements being divided and added together.

The output of this algorithm would be:

4 + 5 + 6

The time complexity of this algorithm is O(4^2).

The output of this algorithm would be:

16

The time complexity of this algorithm is O(16).

The time complexity of this algorithm is O(n log n).

32
